name: fetch_url
summary: Fetch a URL over HTTPS and return its content as text.
HTML is converted to readable plain text; JSON, plain text, and markdown are returned as-is.
constraints: HTTP URLs are upgraded to HTTPS automatically.
Cross-host redirects are surfaced rather than followed: the tool reports the new URL and you re-call with it.
60-second per-request timeout, 10-MB hard cap on response bytes, 10 redirect hops max within the same host.
anti_patterns: For authenticated or private URLs (Nextcloud, GitLab, Confluence, Jira, internal dashboards) — they will fail. Check whether a specialized authenticated tool exists first.
For GitHub URLs — prefer `gh pr view`, `gh issue view`, `gh api` via `bash_tool` for higher fidelity and authentication.
To run an HTTP request with custom headers, methods, or bodies — use `bash_tool` with `curl`.
cautions: IMPORTANT: the tool will fail on authenticated URLs. Do not retry the same URL repeatedly — fall back to a specialized tool or `bash_tool` with proper credentials.
IMPORTANT: when a redirect is detected, the tool returns a `REDIRECT DETECTED` message rather than the page. Make a fresh `fetch_url` call with the redirect URL to actually fetch the content.
output: Plain text. The page body — extracted readable text for HTML, raw payload for non-HTML — truncated to `max_length`.

On a cross-host redirect: a `REDIRECT DETECTED` message naming the original URL, the redirect URL, and the HTTP status. On failure: an error message (validation failure, network error, timeout, body-cap exceeded).

url.description: The URL to fetch. HTTP is upgraded to HTTPS. Max length 2000 characters.

prompt.description: What to extract or focus on. Hint shown alongside the body; does not change what is fetched.

max_length.description: Max response length in characters (default: 100000). Increase only if the content is known to exceed the default.
